text: At the dinner: all in all, Block 1 gave me a strong foundation.

Is that better? Sorry, I was on mute. Would you mind sharing your screen? Pittsburgh cannot see the revenue slide.
Sharing now. Can everyone see it? Maria, you have frozen. I think your line was dropped for a second.
I am back. The call dropped, but I have dialed in again. Could you scroll down to the last quarter?
Of course. By the way, the Pittsburgh team is being muted by the host, so they will type their questions in the chat.

Hi Maria Claudia, it is David in Houston. Quick note before our call this afternoon. Remember we are bringing in the Pittsburgh team this time, so we have three time zones on the line at once. A few things. First, please dial in five minutes early, because last week half the call was lost while people were still logging on. Second, my bandwidth at home is not great, so if my screen freezes or my audio cuts out, just carry on without me and I will dial back in. Third, when it is your turn, would you mind sharing your screen with the revenue slides? And please remember to come off mute before you speak, or we will all be staring at a frozen face. If the connection drops, do not worry; we will simply reschedule the last item for tomorrow. Talk soon.

Alright, two minutes before I dial in, so let me get ready. Three time zones today: Houston, Pittsburgh, and me here in Sao Paulo, which means the call had to be scheduled for my late afternoon. From my side, the most important thing is to be heard clearly, so I have plugged in my headset and closed everything that eats bandwidth. I keep making the same mistake: I start talking while I am still on mute, and then someone has to say Maria, you are being muted. Not today. My plan is simple. I will stay on mute when I am not speaking, I will ask people politely to repeat if the audio gets choppy, and if my screen freezes I will say so instead of pretending. If the line is dropped, I will dial straight back in. Could you give me one clear call, just once? Here we go.

First, you notice the problem: the audio is choppy, or the other person has frozen. Then you flag it politely, with something like could you say that again, or would you mind repeating the last point. Next, you suggest a fix: you ask them to come off mute, to share their screen again, or to turn their camera off to save bandwidth. After that, if the call drops, you stay calm and dial back in without panicking. Finally, if nothing works, you agree to reschedule the rest of the call for a time that suits all three time zones.
